Event: Birth parents give up baby for adoption to ease first-class travel seating
Explanation: They keep their lifestyle convenience while seriously harming their child’s life prospects and emotional well‑being.

Event: Birth parents decide to have another child instead of reclaiming Jared, giving new child the same name
Explanation: They choose an easier replacement child and symbolically erase Jared, compounding his loss without cost to themselves.

Event: Jared becomes Richard’s roommate after squatters take over his apartment
Explanation: He gains housing and proximity to Richard while providing Richard with a roommate/ally.

Event: Squatters take over Jared’s apartment
Explanation: Squatters obtain free housing while seriously harming Jared’s living situation and finances.

Event: Jared lets desire to be around Richard turn him into a “craven yes-man,” clouding judgment and leading Richard “down a horrible path”
Explanation: His neediness undermines his own judgment and enables harmful decisions for Richard with little benefit to anyone.

Event: Jared constantly apologizes and self‑deprecates, tolerating virtually any abuse
Explanation: He harms his own standing and mental health while making it easier for others to mistreat him without real gain to them.

Event: Jared calls Gavin’s blood boy a dick three times for disagreeing with Richard’s idea
Explanation: He vents loyalty‑driven anger in a way that damages his own professionalism and needlessly insults the other person.

Event: Jared reluctantly agrees to unethical plan to hack Pied Piper onto all Hooli Con visitors’ phones because it’s “for the greater good”
Explanation: He compromises his ethics and risks consequences while supporting a privacy‑violating scheme that harms unsuspecting users.

Event: Richard hacks his ex‑girlfriend’s new boyfriend’s screensaver, ruining the Hooli Con mass‑install plan
Explanation: He sabotages the company’s big (if unethical) launch for petty jealousy, hurting everyone including himself.

Event: Jared angrily confronts Richard over ruining their Hooli Con plan
Explanation: He risks tension with Richard but asserts boundaries and forces Richard to face the damage done.

Event: Peter Gregory’s self‑driving car accidentally “kidnaps” Jared to a floating island
Explanation: The malfunction provides no clear benefit while severely inconveniencing Jared.

Event: Jared chooses to take a break from dating to focus energy on Pied Piper
Explanation: He forgoes short‑term romantic pleasure to invest effort into the company’s success, which helps both him and the team.

Event: Jared booby‑traps the house with corporate resources
Explanation: He increases security/control for his and the company’s benefit while introducing risk or discomfort for others.

Event: Jared hits Melcher harshly with his shoe to defend Richard after Richard slept with Melcher’s fiancée
Explanation: He protects Richard emotionally but escalates conflict and legal/personal risk for himself with little net gain.

Event: Jared persistently cares for and supports colleagues despite their open contempt
Explanation: His nurturing behavior benefits team cohesion and ultimately earns him more acceptance without serious self‑harm beyond some stress.

Event: Jared becomes extremely jealous of Holden, wanting to keep Richard dependent on him
Explanation: His possessiveness undermines his own emotional health and creates tension toward Holden without real advantage.

Event: Holden mysteriously disappears shortly after Jared returns to Pied Piper (implied Jared involvement)
Explanation: If he engineered it, he removes a rival to secure his role while seriously harming Holden’s opportunity and agency.

Event: Jared lies to parents of a toddler about having a GPS app tracking their child, using a kidnapping‑style pitch
Explanation: The predatory lie terrifies parents, damages trust, and brings police attention, with negligible or negative gain to him.

Event: Jared tells police an underage child gave him Adderall after he lured the child into his house
Explanation: He incriminates himself as a predator and drug recipient while deepening suspicion and harm toward the child in the story he tells.

Event: Jared rents out his condo on Airbnb while living in Erlich’s garage to cover his mortgage
Explanation: He sacrifices comfort to avoid financial collapse and offers cheap housing, benefiting both sides overall.

Event: Airbnb renter refuses to leave Jared’s condo once Jared wants to move back in
Explanation: The renter gains extended housing for free while imposing financial and legal hardship on Jared.

Event: After the squatter leaves, Jared continues living in the garage out of solidarity
Explanation: He forgoes improved living conditions for symbolic loyalty that mostly benefits others and his image more than his own well‑being.

Event: Richard nominates Jared to replace Erlich on Pied Piper’s board
Explanation: This promotion greatly advances Jared’s status and uses his competence to strengthen the company.

Event: Jared accepts promotion to Chief Operating Officer and serves in that role
Explanation: He gains authority and career advancement while providing strong operational leadership to the company.

Event: Jared makes clear he will follow Richard wherever he goes
Explanation: His loyalty gives Richard crucial emotional and practical support while giving Jared purpose and continued employment.

Event: Richard decides to hijack Hooli Con to upload Pied Piper code onto users’ phones without consent
Explanation: He seeks massive distribution for himself and the company at the expense of users’ privacy and autonomy.

Event: Richard’s subsequent unethical decisions strain Jared’s loyalty and lead Jared to quit Pied Piper
Explanation: Richard’s behavior drives away a key ally, harming Jared’s career stability and the company while only serving his ego.

Event: Jared decides to quit Pied Piper over conflicts with Richard’s unethical decisions
Explanation: He protects his integrity but sacrifices position and influence, modestly harming both himself and the company.

Event: Jared tells Richard he’d do anything to get him back, including killing others or driving them to suicide
Explanation: His extreme declaration reveals dangerous obsession, worsening his own image and threatening others without concrete benefit.

Event: Jared fails to correct Gavin Belson after being mistakenly called “Jared,” adopting the wrong name out of fear
Explanation: He preserves short‑term safety but loses his real name and identity, mainly harming himself while leaving others unaffected.
